headline: GTA 6 Pre-Orders Arrive Next Week, Here's What We've Seen So Far
dek: Rockstar Games just dropped the official cover art, new screenshots, and fresh gameplay footage alongside the pre-order date. Here's a quick hit on all the details you need to know.
body_markdown:
The gates are about to open: Grand Theft Auto 6 pre-orders are officially slated to go live next week, on June 25th, 2026. This announcement, confirmed by Rockstar Games, marks the moment players can finally secure their copy of the highly anticipated title, kicking off what's sure to be a record-setting pre-sales window.

Alongside the pre-order confirmation, Rockstar lifted the veil on the official cover art for GTA 6. While we won't spoil the specifics here, it offers a strong first impression of the game's core aesthetic and hints at key figures players will encounter in Vice City.

But the reveals didn't stop there. We also received a new screenshot, painting a vivid picture of a "beautiful, bustling nighttime scene by the water," as reported by GameSpot. This visual sneak peek gives a tangible sense of the game world's atmosphere and detail. Further cementing the scale of the upcoming title, new official gameplay footage has been released, showcasing the "enormous map" of Vice City, according to ScreenRant.

Even before its November launch, GTA 6 is already making waves far beyond the gaming world. GameRant reports that the game's impending release has reportedly compelled one car company to grant its employees time off specifically to experience the game. This anecdote underscores the profound cultural impact GTA 6 is poised to have, demonstrating its ability to ripple through industries and alter daily routines even before it arrives on shelves.

sources.title: GameRant - "GTA 6 Preorders Officially Go Live Next Week"
GameSpot - "GTA 6 Preorders Begin June 25, Cover Art Revealed; Take-Two Stock Jumps On The News"
GameSpot - "New GTA 6 Screenshot Shows Off A Beautiful, Bustling Nighttime Scene By The Water"
ScreenRant - "GTA 6 Official Gameplay Footage Shows Off Enormous Map"
GameRant - "Upcoming GTA 6 Release Forces Car Company to 'Pause Operations'"
